summary: Bulk include-cleanup PR described as running IWYU and deprecating `WIP`. The PR body notes this was a broad operation expected to need future tweaking, and that automatic linting/fixing of includes would be implemented later. The slice contains no changed-file list, diff, comments, or reviews, so exact header edits and symbol-level impacts are not evidenced here.
what_changed: Ran IWYU, implying a project-wide include cleanup pass.
Deprecated the `WIP` mechanism or marker, based on the PR title.
Set expectation that the bulk include changes may require subsequent manual tweaks.
Deferred automatic linting and include-fixing automation to later work.
smart_moves: Used IWYU as a systematic way to improve include hygiene rather than relying only on manual header edits.
Explicitly acknowledged the bulk nature of the operation and the likelihood of follow-up tweaks, which is important for large decompilation-tree header changes.
Separated the immediate cleanup from future automation, avoiding overloading this PR with both include churn and lint/fix infrastructure.
decomp_lessons: Large include-cleanup passes should be treated as iterative maintenance work; expect some follow-up adjustment after IWYU output lands.
For decompilation projects with many interdependent C files and headers, include hygiene can be improved in bulk, but exact dependency correctness still needs review over time.
When deprecating project-wide markers such as `WIP`, keep evidence in titles/bodies explicit because changed-file context may be too broad or unavailable in later archival slices.
Automatic include linting/fixing was not part of this PR, so future contributors should not assume this merge added enforcement tooling.

naming_conventions.meaning: Include What You Use; used here as the named include-cleanup approach.
A deprecated project marker or mechanism according to the PR title; exact implementation details are not present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find subsequent PRs or commits that tweaked IWYU fallout from PR #1109.
Search for later implementation of automatic linting and fixing of includes.
Search repository history for removal or replacement of `WIP` markers after this PR.
Inspect the full diff for PR #1109 outside this slice to identify exact headers and files affected.
